title: Intelligence Community OSINT Strategy 2024–2026
org: Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI)
notes: Authoritative public US IC strategy; frames OSINT as foundational intelligence discipline and emphasizes governance, discovery, AI/data, and tradecraft.

title: Open Source Enterprise
notes: Public description of CIA open-source mission and all-source integration role.

title: Berkeley Protocol on Digital Open Source Investigations
org: OHCHR / UC Berkeley Human Rights Center
notes: Canonical methodology for lawful, ethical, verifiable digital open-source investigations; excellent evidence handling and chain-of-custody guidance.
url_status: curl_403_or_bot_blocked; public OHCHR landing page used

title: Berkeley Protocol publication page
org: Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights
notes: Stable landing page for the Berkeley Protocol.
url_status: curl_403_or_bot_blocked; public OHCHR landing page used

title: Open-source internet research
notes: Public UK policing guidance on internet research, proportionality, authorization, risk, and recordkeeping.

title: Internet intelligence and investigations
notes: Broader public UK guidance surrounding online investigative activity and governance.

title: Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) 2024
notes: Useful for contemporary digital threat context and public cybercrime trend awareness; not a methodology manual.

title: ENISA Threat Landscape 2024
notes: Public threat landscape; supports lawful cyber/technical OSINT context and source triangulation.

title: Guidelines 8/2020 on the targeting of social media users
notes: Important privacy/data protection boundary for social media data collection and processing.

title: Guide to the UK GDPR
notes: Privacy/legal boundary source for handling personal data found through OSINT.

title: Companies House Find and update company information
notes: Primary-source corporate registry; model for public-record verification.

title: SEC EDGAR Full Text Search
notes: Primary-source corporate filings; relevant to entity OSINT, financial disclosures, and source hierarchy.

title: National Sex Offender Public Website
notes: Official public registry; illustrates sensitive-record handling and official-source priority.

title: OFAC Sanctions List Search
org: US Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control
notes: Primary sanctions list source; useful for entity/name resolution and international records.

title: EU Sanctions Map
notes: EU official sanctions source; use for primary-source verification.

title: Canada Business Registries search
org: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
notes: Canadian corporate registry search; useful for public-record OSINT.

title: Australian Business Register ABN Lookup
notes: Australian business identifier registry; primary source for entity verification.

title: New Zealand Companies Register
notes: NZ corporate registry; supports cross-jurisdiction public-record workflows.

title: EUvsDisinfo methodology
notes: Public methodology for classifying and documenting disinformation cases; useful for verification taxonomy.

title: NATO Strategic Communications Centre of Excellence publications
notes: Public research on information environment, influence operations, and digital platforms; use selectively for OSINT context.

title: OSCE Representative on Freedom of the Media publications
notes: Useful for journalist safety, media freedom, and verification context; not OSINT-specific by itself.

title: The Not Yet Exploited Goldmine of OSINT: Opportunities, Open Challenges and Future Trends
notes: Survey of OSINT opportunities, challenges, and trends; useful for taxonomy and automation limits.

title: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT): Issues for Congress
notes: Policy-oriented overview of OSINT definitions, governance, and IC issues.

title: Information Disorder: Toward an interdisciplinary framework for research and policymaking
org: Wardle and Derakhshan / Council of Europe
notes: Canonical misinformation/disinformation taxonomy: misinformation, disinformation, malinformation; useful for verification sections.

title: Defining “Fake News”: A typology of scholarly definitions
org: Tandoc, Lim, and Ling, Digital Journalism
notes: Clarifies categories of false/misleading content; helps avoid sloppy labels.

title: The spread of true and false news online
notes: Evidence on diffusion of false news; supports caution when using social signals as evidence.

title: Misinformation and Its Correction: Continued Influence and Successful Debiasing
org: Lewandowsky et al., Psychological Science in the Public Interest
notes: Useful for analyst-bias and correction sections.

title: Fighting misinformation on social media using crowdsourced judgments of news source quality
notes: Source-quality judgments and crowd approaches; relevant to reliability but not sufficient alone.

title: The science of fake news
notes: Broad research agenda for online misinformation; supports platform-data limitations and governance awareness.

title: OSINT as Part of Cyber Defense: systematic and survey literature
notes: Search route for cyber OSINT papers; use to ground lawful technical reconnaissance, attack-surface awareness, and automation caveats.

title: Social Media Intelligence: privacy and methodological concerns
notes: Search route for social media intelligence literature; flag as search route, not a single source.

title: Digital Open Source Investigations: methodological and evidentiary research
notes: Search route complementing Berkeley Protocol; useful for future expansion.

title: Bellingcat Online Investigation Toolkit
notes: Curated practical OSINT tool/resource list; use as tool landscape, not sole authority.

title: Bellingcat Guides and Resources
notes: Practice articles on geolocation, verification, archiving, and investigative workflows.

title: Online Research Tools
notes: Journalism-oriented OSINT tool list and method route.

title: The Verification Handbook
notes: Canonical verification workflow for user-generated content and online claims.

title: First Draft Essential Guide to Verifying Online Information
notes: Strong verification practices; note First Draft is no longer active but resources remain influential.

title: OCCRP Aleph
org: Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project
notes: Investigative public/leaked-record search platform; use with source/context caution.

title: ICIJ Offshore Leaks Database
notes: Public searchable database for offshore leaks; requires careful identity resolution and context.

title: Hunchly: OSINT workflow and capture tool resources
notes: Good for evidence capture and browser-based workflow practices; treat vendor content as practice-oriented, not neutral authority.

title: Maltego blog and resources
notes: Link-analysis and entity-resolution practice examples; vendor caveat applies.

title: Shodan Help Center
notes: Useful for lawful internet-exposure discovery and query syntax; avoid exploit usage.

title: SpiderFoot documentation
notes: Automation tool docs; useful for describing collection automation, API keys, false positives, and scoping.

title: OSINT Framework
notes: Broad tool map; useful as discovery index, not authority.

title: OpenCorporates
notes: Corporate registry aggregator; verify critical findings against primary registries.

title: crt.sh Certificate Search
notes: Certificate transparency search for domains/subdomains; useful for public infrastructure OSINT.

title: Internet Archive Wayback Machine
notes: Core archiving and historical-web source; record snapshot dates and limitations.

title: Have I Been Pwned
notes: Lawful breach-exposure checking without raw dump access; mainly self-audit/defensive.

title: Recorded Future threat intelligence resources
notes: Useful public reports on threat intelligence and OSINT-adjacent practices; vendor caveat.

title: Flashpoint resources
notes: Public reports can inform risk framing and collection categories; vendor caveat and avoid dark-web operational detail.
